generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE contract syntax and visible symbol usage, not altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Prioritize resolving identifier and type errors reported by the AGREE validator before attempting more complex repairs.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helper variables within AGREE contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed within an `annex agree { ... };` block.
Use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, and `const` keywords for AGREE contract elements.
Ensure type consistency between expressions and declarations (int, real, bool).
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new AADL architecture to resolve AGREE contract errors.
Avoid using implementation-specific details (e.g., subcomponent paths) in component type contracts.
Do not attempt to repair contracts with unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations without first addressing the root cause.
irrelevant_topics: AADL structural extraction details (component types, implementations, features, connections, properties, owners)
AGREE contract placement in component type vs. implementation scopes
